response: The disc jockey whose birth name is Tony Blackburn is the one who notably used Mark Wirtz's song "A Touch of Velvet, A Sting of Brass" on their Radio Caroline show. Tony Blackburn, born on 29 January 1943, was a disc jockey who broadcast on the "pirate" stations Radio Caroline and Radio London in the 1960s. He was the second disc jockey to broadcast on BBC Radio 1 at its launch in 1967. Blackburn was known for his work on Radio Caroline, where he featured Wirtz's song as the theme tune for the Radio Bremen show Musikladen.
